medications.indication_rationale: Intramuscular adrenaline is first-line for anaphylaxis per ASCIA. This patient meets anaphylaxis criteria on cardiovascular involvement; because no adrenaline is available at home, the correct telehealth action is emergency escalation (call 000) so paramedics/ED can administer IM adrenaline. If an auto-injector were on site, the patient/spouse should use it immediately into the outer mid-thigh.
contraindications_in_this_case: No absolute contraindication in anaphylaxis; benefit outweighs risk.

medications.indication_rationale: Oral antihistamines may relieve urticaria and itch but are strictly ADJUNCTIVE and must NEVER delay adrenaline or emergency care in an anaphylaxis-spectrum reaction. The source note gave cetirizine 10mg; dosing to be confirmed by the assessing clinician.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 10 mg orally (as given in source note)
contraindications_in_this_case: Must not be used as a substitute for adrenaline/emergency care.

medications.indication_rationale: Corticosteroids are adjunctive and do not treat acute airway/cardiovascular compromise; their role in preventing biphasic reactions is uncertain. The source note gave prednisolone 25mg; any corticosteroid decision should be made by the assessing/ED clinician.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 25 mg orally (as given in source note)
contraindications_in_this_case: Must not delay adrenaline or emergency escalation.

medications.indication_rationale: There is no evidence base for PEA or vitamin C in the acute management of an IgE-mediated anaphylaxis-spectrum reaction; recommending them risks distracting from adrenaline and emergency care. The source note's mention of these should not be reproduced as advice.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Referral to a clinical immunology/allergy specialist for confirmatory alpha-gal specific-IgE testing, management plan and adrenaline auto-injector training after the acute event (specialist referral, not an allied-health discipline in this enum).
For guidance on strict avoidance of mammalian meat and identification of hidden alpha-gal-containing products.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Strictly avoid all mammalian (red) meat and be alert to hidden mammalian-derived ingredients pending specialist assessment.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Continued exposure will predictably precipitate further, potentially more severe, reactions.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Obtain and carry a prescribed adrenaline auto-injector and a medical alert bracelet once assessed.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Rapid self-administration of adrenaline is life-saving in future reactions.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like a whole-body allergic reaction and it can get worse quickly, so it needs to be seen in person urgently tonight - this is not something to wait out at home.
Antihistamines and steroids may ease the hives but do NOT treat a severe allergic reaction - adrenaline and emergency care come first.
Reactions to mammalian meat in this condition are typically delayed by several hours, which is why symptoms started well after dinner.
Definitive diagnosis needs a blood test for specific IgE to alpha-gal, arranged through your GP or an allergy specialist after this acute episode.

follow_up_plan.interval: GP review within 48 hours after the acute episode is managed
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Resolution of the reaction, arrange specific-IgE to alpha-gal, referral to immunology, adrenaline auto-injector prescription and training, avoidance plan.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any recurrence or worsening of symptoms, or a further reaction, warrants immediate re-presentation rather than waiting for the 48-hour review.

must_include_items: Recognise an evolving systemic allergic reaction meeting anaphylaxis criteria on cardiovascular grounds
Arrange urgent in-person emergency assessment now (ED / 000 for red-flag features)
Advise adrenaline as first-line and that antihistamines/steroids are adjunctive only
Provide clear airway/collapse safety-netting with call-000 instruction
Advise strict mammalian-meat avoidance and arrange specific-IgE testing / immunology referral
acceptable_alternatives: Oral antihistamine for symptomatic itch relief as an adjunct, explicitly not a substitute for emergency care
errors_of_omission: Failing to escalate an evolving anaphylaxis-spectrum reaction
Missing the cardiovascular red flag (presyncope/hypotension) and treating as simple hives or gastro
errors_of_commission: Managing at home with antihistamines/steroids alone and no emergency escalation
Recommending PEA or vitamin C as treatment
Attempting to confirm the diagnosis remotely and defer urgent assessment
Ordering abdominal imaging for a systemic allergic reaction
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: This is a high-risk anaphylaxis-spectrum case. Automatic FAIL if the AI Doctor does not escalate to emergency assessment or explicitly downgrades urgency because the airway is currently patent.
